plan: Trim SPY to reduce concentration and comply with the single-name limit.
Sell all leveraged ETFs, SOXL and SPAL, to eliminate high-risk exposure.
Reduce SMH exposure, as it is flagged for trimming and in a downtrend.
Increase QQQI to boost income and broad index exposure for stability.
Buy MSFT as a top-quality name with a strong scanner read.
Establish a 15% cash buffer to enhance capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a ~1.5%/month outlook with a focus on capital preservation. Expect significantly lower volatility and reduced drawdown risk, but also limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the single-name concentration limit and reduce gross leverage.
Reduce SOXL exposure and sell SPAL to manage overall leveraged ETF risk.
Trim SMH as it is flagged for reduction and in a downtrend.
Slightly trim QQQI, which is flagged for trimming, to rebalance.
Buy MSFT to add a high-quality tech name with a strong scanner read.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets a ~2.5%/month outlook, prioritizing risk-first growth. Expect moderate volatility and drawdown potential, balancing growth with prudent risk management.

moves.note: Add strong quality tech, high score

plan: Trim SPY to meet the single-name concentration limit and reduce gross leverage.
Increase SOXL exposure, leaning into leveraged semiconductors with strict stops.
Sell SPAL, as it is underperforming and below its stop.
Reduce SMH exposure, as it is flagged for trimming and in a downtrend.
Increase CRWD, which is performing well and in an uptrend.
Buy ZS and MSFT, both high-scoring scanner setups, to add growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets a ~4%/month outlook, aiming for stretch growth. Expect significantly higher volatility and potential for larger drawdowns, amplified by leveraged ETFs and concentration. Leverage decay is a daily factor, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to reduce concentration and reallocate to income-generating assets.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta names (SMH, CRWD) to minimize risk.
Significantly increase QQQI exposure for steady monthly distributions.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer for stability and potential yield opportunities.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth potential.
income.read: This income plan targets a ~1%/month outlook, focusing on parking cash for yield. Expect very low drawdown potential and consistent distributions, but limited capital appreciation. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
